Details: Removal of metolachlor degradates from water by coagulation/clarification was found to be ineffective. Based on data from one bench-scale study, 3 to 10 percent removal was reported for the following metolachlor degradates: hydroxy metolachlor, deschloro metolachlor, metolachlor morpholinone, metolachlor propanol, deschloroacetyl metolachlor, and deschloroacetyl metolachlor propanol. No difference was shown based on the type of coagulant used, i.e. aluminum sulfate or ferric chloride. In another study monitoring a full-scale plant, a total 67 to 74 percent removal of the degradates metolachlor sulphonic acid (ME-ESA) and metolachlor oxanilic acid (ME-OXA) was observed for conventional treatment with GAC filters and PAC.
